title: Module 7: Collaborative Climate & Focusing
learning_objective: Create a collaborative rather than authoritative conversational climate; Invite the patient to set priorities collaboratively; Use transparency about time and structure; Balance flexibility with focus
scenario_context: Learn to establish a collaborative partnership and set a shared agenda at the start of conversations about weight loss

persona_config.name: Well,
persona_config.background: Learn to establish a collaborative partnership and set a shared agenda at the start of conversations about weight loss

collab_1.persona_text: Well, I sit at a desk all day for work. By evening I'm too tired to do anything except watch TV. I used to be more active when I was younger.

choice_points.option_text: You need to start exercising at least 30 minutes a day and cut your calorie intake by 500 calories.

feedback.immediate: This directive approach ignores the patient's perspective and imposes an agenda, likely increasing resistance.
feedback.learning_note: Watch for patient resistance signals - defensiveness or disengagement.

choice_points.option_text: You notice quite a contrast between your current lifestyle and how you used to be. What would be most useful to focus on today?

feedback.immediate: This validates the patient's observation and invites them to set priorities, creating a collaborative climate.
feedback.learning_note: Validation acknowledges the patient's experience without judgment.

choice_points.option_text: Let me explain the health risks of being sedentary and overweight. It's very important you understand this.

feedback.immediate: This takes an expert stance that may feel patronizing and doesn't honor patient autonomy.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

collab_2a.persona_text: That sounds like a lot. I don't think I can do all that right now. I barely have time as it is.

choice_points.option_text: If you don't make time, your health will continue to decline. It's really up to you.

feedback.immediate: This sounds like a threat disguised as respecting choice, damaging the relationship.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: I hear that feels overwhelming. Let's step back - what matters most to you about your health right now?

feedback.immediate: Good recovery - acknowledging the discord and returning to collaboration.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

collab_2b.persona_text: I guess I'm most worried about my weight and how tired I feel all the time. But I'm not sure where to start.

choice_points.option_text: Those are connected concerns. We have about 15 minutes today - would it help to explore what might be behind that fatigue?

feedback.immediate: Excellent - makes connections, is transparent about time, and seeks permission to focus.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: The fatigue is probably from being overweight. Let's create an exercise plan.

feedback.immediate: This jumps ahead without exploring the patient's perspective, taking control.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Let's tackle both - your weight and your energy levels. We need to cover diet, exercise, and sleep.

feedback.immediate: Too many topics at once can overwhelm and dilute focus.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

collab_2c.persona_text: I know it's not good for me. I've heard all this before.

choice_points.option_text: You've heard the information before. What would be more helpful for you today?

feedback.immediate: Good pivot - acknowledging without judgment and returning control to the patient.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: Well, hearing it and acting on it are two different things. That's why we need a concrete plan.

feedback.immediate: This sounds critical and maintains the non-collaborative dynamic.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

collab_3b.persona_text: I want to have more energy to play with my kids when I get home from work. That's what really matters to me.

choice_points.option_text: Playing with your kids is really important to you. That's a wonderful motivation. Should we stay with this focus for now?

feedback.immediate: Perfect - affirms values and collaboratively confirms the direction.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: That's great, but we also need to discuss your weight and diet.

feedback.immediate: The 'but' dismisses what the patient just shared as important.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

collab_3c.persona_text: Yes, I think understanding the tiredness would help. Sometimes I wonder if it's just being at a desk all day or something more.

choice_points.option_text: What have you noticed about when you feel most tired versus when you have more energy?

feedback.immediate: Good - stays within the agreed focus and invites the patient's observations.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

choice_points.option_text: It's probably your weight and lack of exercise. Most people feel tired when they're sedentary.

feedback.immediate: This shifts back to an expert stance rather than exploring collaboratively.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

collab_3d.persona_text: That's a lot to think about. I'm not sure I can focus on everything at once.

choice_points.option_text: You're right - that is a lot. What feels like the most important place to start?

feedback.immediate: Good recovery - validates concern and invites the patient to prioritize.
feedback.learning_note: Validation acknowledges the patient's experience without judgment.

choice_points.option_text: These are all connected, so we need to address them all together.

feedback.immediate: This ignores the patient's stated concern and maintains practitioner control.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

collab_4.persona_text: I feel most tired after sitting all day, but then I also don't have energy to do anything active. It's like a cycle.

choice_points.option_text: You're caught in this cycle where sitting makes you tired, but tiredness keeps you from being active. What might help break that pattern, even in a small way?

feedback.immediate: Excellent - deepens the reflection and continues exploring within the collaborative focus.
feedback.learning_note: Reflections build rapport by showing you understand the patient's perspective.

choice_points.option_text: Actually, we should also talk about your eating habits since those affect energy too.

feedback.immediate: This abandons the agreed focus without checking with the patient first.
feedback.learning_note: Continue practicing MI techniques to build your therapeutic skills.

collab_end_good.persona_text: Maybe I could try taking a short walk during lunch? Even just 10 minutes to move around.

collab_end_poor.persona_text: I don't think this is helping. Maybe I'll think about it and come back another time.
